mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.5%, tight labor markets accelerate wage growth and consumer spending, driving upward earnings revisions and lifting equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative or flat 20d returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, or the predictive Rank IC drops below 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which historically precedes positive equity returns as corporate profit margins widen.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or cross-sectional IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of rising inflation expectations, the market prices in future Fed tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation episodes, or the correlation between CPIAUCSL momentum and forward returns flips to positive over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic momentum that translates into higher corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.0% annualized, or Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production growth accelerates past 0.5% month-over-month, it indicates a supply-led recovery that boosts industrial and materials equities, lifting the broader index over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO crosses the 0.5% MoM threshold, or predictive power vanishes over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances coupled with stable or falling short-term interest rates indicate accommodative financial conditions that fuel discretionary spending and equity risk appetite over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and stable FEDFUNDS fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term in a regression model yields a statistically insignificant coefficient.

mechanism_narrative: During periods where payroll employment growth exceeds 200,000 per month while housing starts decline, it signals a labor-driven consumption boom that eventually overheats the economy, leading to negative equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of periods where PAYEMS exceeds 200k monthly additions, or the historical correlation between strong payrolls and subsequent equity returns flips to positive.
